title: centaurea ovina subsp. koktebelica
intro: Centaurea ovina subsp. koktebelica, also known as a subspecies of the Kerguelen cabbage thistle or Kerguelen blue thistle, is a plant in the sunflower family that is native to the Crimean Peninsula. The plant has been reported to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Centaurea ovina subsp. koktebelica include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Centaurea ovina subsp. koktebelica can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
